architecture_fit.note: Minimal base_model delta (2L/4H attn-only, MLP dropped); L0H0 nails the defined t-1 signature (excess 0.81) but is a pure positional prev-token head that ignores HMM content.

baseline_comparison.note: Per-head bar plus uniform(0)/random(-0.001) baselines show only L0H0 beats a uniform reader; sibling heads (self/BOS/uniform) are explicit strawmen that fail.

faithfulness.note: Ablating L0H0 or zeroing the positional encoding collapses excess to ~0 while every other head leaves it intact; honest that for a hand-built circuit this is causal-by-construction and proposes activation patching for a trained model.

operating_range.note: Temperature sweep spans ~2 orders of magnitude (collapses to 0 at temp→0) and seq-len 8→28 degrades gracefully; seed sweep is identical, which honestly reflects the head's content-independence rather than added coverage.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire circuit is hand-set (one-hot positional code + W_Q reading code(t-1)); reproduces the target behaviour with zero training — strong mechanism understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Four focused panels (per-head bar, positional line, ablation barh, operating-range triptych) plus a selectable attention heatmap, each mapping one claim to the benchmark quantity on the y-axis; app.py is structurally sound.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties every chart to a specific rubric question with the benchmark's excess metric on the y-axis and the 0 baseline drawn in.

notes: A clean hand-built attempt: a minimal attention-only delta from base_model.py whose L0H0 is a positional previous-token head that maxes the goal's defined t-1 excess metric (0.81 vs 0 uniform), with strong baseline, ablation, and operating-range panels and an exemplary visualisation. Its main limitation is that the head is purely positional and ignores HMM observations entirely (the seed sweep is bit-identical), so it demonstrates the Viterbi attention *substrate* rather than a trained model implementing the recurrence — which the README states honestly. Solidly good across the rubric, with full marks on the hardcoded-weights bonus and visualisation items.
